response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist with deep web, backend, and systems experience, but the profile does not match this role's core requirements. His C++ exposure is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, far from the mandated 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience listed (MongoDB/MySQL instead), and no meaningful fixed-income or capital-markets domain work — RIDE Capital was investment-adjacent but built on Node/TypeScript, not C++ trading systems.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems background (DLang async I/O services handling millions of requests/sec)
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling authorship
Broad polyglot experience and demonstrated ability to learn new stacks quickly
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience, and not recent (2014–2015)
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary databases are MongoDB and MySQL
No fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain expertise; no Summit platform exposure

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and performance-systems skills
